original_reasoning: Arabic 'wrinkled/folded belly' matches Greek 'helix/spiral' well through the shared core of folds and coils.
Arabic ghamal = accumulated (trees/clouds); Greek Gamala = Gamla in Golan Heights, Semitic place name. Shared Semitic substrate for place name plausible.
Greek 'well-aimed' links clearly to Arabic slaughter/victim through striking the target and the result.
Arabic describes a large fleshy woman, which aligns clearly with the Greek idea of thick-skinned or thick-bodied form.
Arabic eating and drinking strongly fit a bowl or basin as the vessel that receives drink or food.
Mashaj - to mix between two vs 'to mix' - direct match on mixing
Arabic 'hairy' and Greek 'thistle' meet through a rough spiny-hairy surface concept.
Arabic qirdim = 'pomegranate peel used for tanning; one who grabs'; Greek = 'garden cress/cardamom'. Clear Mediterranean loanword connection (cardamom was traded across Arabia).
